title: Cotswolds: Great Rissington (Ashespring), Bourton-on-the-Water

main_route.excerpt: Simon, Eve + Tinder left just after 6. We arrived about 7.45 at Great Rissington, Ashespring
Then tea, then we all left. The first time we had to stop on the return journey was at the Green Man red light in Harborne.

stops.excerpt: After lunch we walked into Bourton-on-the-Water. Very pretty. The aim of the trip was to have an icecream, which we did.

summary: A short Cotswolds weekend in late June 2014, with Simon, Eve and Tinder based at the Ashespring campsite in Great Rissington; Charles and his partner Jenny also joined for part of the stay. Day trips included a walk into Bourton-on-the-Water, and the family returned to Birmingham via Harborne on Sunday evening.
extraction_notes: The transcription metadata gives an ISO date of 2003-06-27, but June 27th fell on a Friday in 2014 (matching the diary wording 'Friday June 27th') not in 2003 - the trip_id year of 2014 is used. The diary covers Friday 27th (arrival), Saturday 28th (Bourton-on-the-Water excursion), and Sunday 29th (further walks, Morgan 3-wheeler, departure). Harborne is a suburb of Birmingham, confirming return home on Sunday evening. Charles is listed as a son in the family; Jenny is his partner and not part of the core family list. No ferry crossings - entirely within England.
